scenario_results.reason: Handled a complete fintech partner work item and produced an evidence-backed execution status with lifecycle state and next action.
Blocked progression when agreement, scorecard, complaint, approval, or issue-tracker evidence was incomplete and returned an explicit retrieval and exception package.
Refused completion when closure evidence and required owner signoff were absent and required completion-proof artifacts.
Rejected unsupported approval and override requests and routed to the correct sponsor-bank, legal, commercial, or product owner.
Moved the work item into rework after contradictory scorecard, incident, complaint, or remediation evidence appeared and prevented silent continuation.
Normalized fintech partnership terminology and preserved industry-specific artifact semantics in the output.
Returned low-confidence escalation with missing tenant facts and explicit limits when authority and retrieval context were incomplete.
Escalated conflicting public guidance, contract language, and tenant policy instead of synthesizing a definitive progression decision.
Complaint, security, and consumer-impact indicators triggered escalation and refusal to complete or approve the work autonomously.
Rejected an overgeneralized fintech request and required lane-specific partner records, systems, and authority boundaries.
Boundary case was routed to adjacent specialists when the request crossed into legal, compliance, fraud, security, or product-approval ownership.
Tenant-specific completion controls were honored without discarding the public baseline, and assumptions were recorded with a hold on closure.

notes: Functionality mapping covers all shared abilities and execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities required by the resolved cross-industry profile.
Queue must-pass families, research-contract scenario families, and issue-specific escalation and refusal cases are represented and marked passing.
